title: Failure Design, Defer, Panic, Recover, and Resource Ownership · Execution Trace: Error Contract Design
summary: A multi-stage import must close owned resources, preserve partial-progress policy, and avoid converting programming defects into success. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go error contract design increment in a resource-safe import workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Design error contracts that state operation, relevant identity, cause, retry meaning, and partial-result policy.
Place interfaces at consumer boundaries, return concrete useful types, and evaluate mocks, adapters, and evolution cost.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
